impacts.statement: Mini shopping center on WV State Route 16 adjacent to Philip Run floods due to backwater flooding.
Homes along South River Street and Simon Street flood.

Homes along River Street behind the old high school flood. Homes and businesses between River Street and Mill Street flood at 40 to 42 feet. This includes Court Street, Hardman Street, Florence Street, Elizabeth Street, and Margaret Street.
Homes along River Street between the intersections with Hardman and Mill Streets flood.
Gas utility company on Northside Road next to gage floods.
Homes along WV State Route 5 north of Grantsville that are adjacent to the river flood.
Homes along North River Street between Main Street and Mill Street flood. Baseball field on School Street floods.

Behind old high school, homes along River Street flood at 40 feet

Homes and businesses along South River and Simon Streets flood at 40 to 41 feet

hydronotes.statement: River Forecasts typically include ONLY 2 days of future rain from <u>forecast issuance time</u> (lower right of graph). Occasionally 3 days of future rain included.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.
